GreenComp Toolkit

EU sustainability competence framework · 12 competences
Which best describes you?

The toolkit adapts to your role. You can switch anytime.

✓ 100% offline ✓ GDPR-safe ✓ DE / EN ✓ 12 competences
How to use the toolkit in 3 steps
The European sustainability competence framework

GreenComp · 12 competences for sustainable thinking, planning and acting

GreenComp is the EU's sustainability competence framework developed by the Joint Research Centre (JRC). 4 areas × 3 competences each = 12 sustainability competences for learners and educators across Europe.

🌿 Before the 12 competences, a moment.

Sustainability doesn't begin with data — it begins with the sense of being part of something larger.

0
Competences
0
Competence areas
0
EU member states
0
EU climate target year
0
Offline · no upload
"

GreenComp is a reference framework for sustainability competences that empowers learners to think, plan, and act with empathy, responsibility, and care for our planet and for public health.

— Joint Research Centre · European Commission, GreenComp Publication (2022)
Why it matters
1
A common language
Sustainability moves from buzzword to teachable competence — across all 27 EU education systems.
2
Evidence for EU reporting
Pre/Post snapshots become measurable evidence aligned with Erasmus+ Green Strategy 2024 and the Union of Skills + Preparedness Union priorities.
3
Beyond green-only
The 12 competences map to inclusion, digital, democracy, wellbeing — every Erasmus+ priority builds skill here.
Policy anchoring

GreenComp is referenced in the 2024 Erasmus+ Green Transition Strategy and the 2022 Council Recommendation on learning for environmental sustainability. It supports the Union of Skills and Preparedness Union priorities (Commission, March 2025).

Sources: 📘 GreenComp · JRC128040 📄 Council Recommendation 2022 🎓 Erasmus+ priorities 🇪🇺 Union of Skills 🛡️ Preparedness Union
The 12 at a glance · interactive

Click any quadrant to see its 3 competences. Click a competence for the full detail. A faster way to navigate than scrolling — and a teaching aid in itself.

The Bee Metaphor — the official JRC visual representation
"

As an analogy of a highly developed natural system, the metaphor stresses the interaction and dynamic between the four areas and the 12 competences of GreenComp.

— JRC128040 · The European sustainability competence framework, Section 3.1, p. 16

The JRC describes the four areas through the bee-pollination metaphor — each part of the natural cycle stands for one area of competences:

🏠
Beehive = Values
The hive protects and sustains the bees — like values protect what matters most.
🌼
Pollen + Nectar = Complexity
Pollen and nectar draw bees from flower to flower — like complexity draws us into systems, evidence and framing.
🌸
Flowers = Futures
Flowers become fruits and fruits seed new life — like envisioning futures allows what comes next.
🐝
Bees = Action
Bees act individually and collectively — each role essential, all working toward the same goal.
View Figure 3 in the original JRC publication
Two personas — one toolkit

The same GreenComp toolkit serves two different roles, each with their own workflow:

National Agency
  1. Share toolkit with TCA participants 2 weeks ahead
  2. Collect Pre-TCA CSVs from each participant
  3. Run TCA · use methods from Method Library
  4. Collect Post-TCA CSVs
  5. Drop all CSVs in Aggregator → impact report
Use for: TCA evaluation, EU strategic-priority evidence, annual NA reporting
TCA Participant
  1. Open the toolkit, read 12 competences in Framework
  2. Self-rate 1–5 — save Pre-TCA snapshot
  3. Export CSV, email to your NA
  4. Attend TCA · reflect using competence questions
  5. Re-rate afterwards — save Post-TCA + export CSV
Use for: personal growth tracking, peer-comparison, learning evidence

12 competences, 4 areas

The radial Atlas below is the centrepiece. Click any competence node for full detail, or filter by area. Below the Atlas: deeper area cards with persona-specific tips.

Interactive Atlas — 12 competences in radial map
The 4 areas in depth

Self-assessment

Rate yourself (participant context) or benchmark your team (organiser context). Save Pre-TCA and Post-TCA snapshots to measure impact.

National Agency preview
This is exactly what your participants see and fill in.
The 1–5 self-ratings, the Pre/Post snapshots, your reflection questions and the “send to NA” buttons further down — your participants get all of this. Try it yourself to understand their journey, then hand them a locked Participant edition. Their data always stays on their own device.
A note on the 1–5 scale (please read once)
GreenComp itself (JRC128040) is a descriptive reference framework — it does NOT define formal, certified proficiency levels. The 1–5 scale used here is a self-reflection aid, not an objective measurement or a certifiable competence rating. Use it to make your own growth visible to yourself and to document trends across a TCA. Treat aggregated numbers as conversation starters, not as audit-grade evidence.
How self-assessment works · 3 steps
1
Read & rate (be honest)
For each of the 12 competences below, pick a level 1–5. Use the legend or click any competence for full description, KSA and behavioural anchors per level.
→
2
Save Pre-TCA snapshot
Once rated, click 'Save Pre-TCA' (below) to freeze this moment. This is your honest baseline before any training happens.
→
3
After TCA · re-rate + save Post
Return after the TCA, re-rate your levels, click 'Save Post-TCA'. The delta = your impact. Export CSV in header → mail to NA. Done.
Your details (optional)

Used to label your snapshots + CSV so your NA knows whose data it is. Optional — leave blank for anonymous.

What do the levels mean?
1
Not yet — I can't practice this skill
2
Aware — I understand, need guidance
3
Applied — I've applied it successfully
4
Confident — I apply it regularly
5
Expert — I can train others
Snapshots
Feedback to your NA (optional)

Free text — what worked, what you'd change, anything else. Included in your export and mail.

Your results — save or send

Tip: you can also save your results as a CSV and send that to your NA — see 'Advanced' just below.

Advanced — CSV & other formats

TCA Mapping Tool

Describe any TCA topic — green or not — and see which GreenComp competences it connects to.

Saved sessions
Saves your whole working state (self-assessment, snapshots, TCA plan, methods, participant) under a name — like the Risikomatrix tool. Stays local in your browser; export JSON for backup.
🗺️
1 Pick your competences

Mix sources freely — every pick lands in your plan on the right.

★ = one of the 4 official Erasmus+ horizontal priorities. The others are common TCA themes that also map to GreenComp.
2 My TCA Plan

Pick from any source on the left — chips land here. Export when ready — print, share, paste into your application.

Print / PDF: If a print or PDF window does not open, allow pop-ups for this page in your browser.
3 Workshop Agendaoptional

Only needed when you actually run the TCA — turns your TCA Plan into a runnable workshop with a timed agenda and matched methods. Skip if you only need the documented plan.

Final step · Create the participant edition
Turns everything above into a tailored offline HTML for your participants — your planned competences become their focus, with event info, language and reflection questions baked in. They self-assess and mail results back to you.

Workshop Method Library

Detailed step-by-step facilitator guides for using GreenComp in TCA sessions.

0 methods selected · click cards to select
How sharing works — methods & questions across NAs
  1. Select one or more methods (tick the cards) and click 'Share methods (.json)' — you get one small file. Reflection questions have their own 'Share questions' button in the Workshop Agenda.
  2. Put the file in your shared Teams folder — e.g. 'GreenComp Tool / Methods' — or any folder your colleagues use.
  3. They click 'Import' in their own copy of the toolkit. The content lands in their library — editable and ready to use. No server, no account; the file contains methods and questions only, no personal data.
  4. Over time the shared folder becomes a growing library — the best methods and questions reach every National Agency.

Competence Gallery

12 spotlight cards — one for each competence. Each card shows how the competence might play out in practice, plus one signature action. Click any gem to dive deeper.

Illustrative scenarios · The short stories on each gem (Helsinki, Porto, Riga…) are fictional but plausible illustrations of what each competence looks like in practice. For two real, JRC-documented case studies — Fatima and Alex — see the cards below the gallery.
Two real use cases from JRC128040 (Annex 1)

In the original JRC GreenComp publication, Annex 1 presents two extended case studies showing all 12 competences in action. Below are short summaries — the full versions are in the JRC PDF (pages 38–41).

1
Fatima — the teacher and the polluted river
Source: JRC128040, Annex 1, pp. 38–39

Fatima is a teacher who wants her pupils to spend more time in nature (Promoting Nature, 1.3). The local river is polluted by industrial waste and household plastic; residents avoid the area. With investigative attitude (Critical Thinking 2.2, Exploratory Thinking 3.3), she decides to treat this as a sustainability problem (Problem Framing 2.3, Valuing Sustainability 1.1). She imagines families picnicking there, schools running field trips, the river used for rowing (Futures Literacy 3.1). She maps direct and indirect causes (Systems Thinking 2.1, Individual Initiative 4.3), brings in city officials (Collective Action 4.2, Political Agency 4.1), stops driving and starts bussing to school (Adaptability 3.2), petitions the school cafeteria to go vegetarian (Political + Collective Action 4.1/4.2), and organises a community treasure hunt to collect waste, with prizes that protect pollinators.

1.1 1.2 1.3 2.1 2.2 2.3 3.1 3.2 3.3 4.1 4.2 4.3
2
Alex — the student, SDG 12 and eco-anxiety
Source: JRC128040, Annex 1, pp. 39–41

Alex, a fourth-year secondary-school student from a different background, has integrated through volunteering (Supporting Fairness 1.2, Individual Initiative 4.3). He helped restore a primary-school green space (Promoting Nature 1.3, Collective Action 4.2). Recently feeling 'eco-anxiety', he joined a school project: Will we reach the SDGs by 2030? His group works on SDG 12 (responsible production/consumption). They research best practice (Systems Thinking 2.1, Critical Thinking 2.2), imagine a future circular community (Futures Literacy 3.1, Valuing Sustainability 1.1), then develop strategy aligned with the waste hierarchy. They plan peer-teaching on recycling (Individual Initiative 4.3), community park cleanups (Collective Action 4.2), letters to the council with photos (Political Agency 4.1), and a clothes-swap circle to reduce fast-fashion consumption (Exploratory Thinking 3.3, Adaptability 3.2).

1.1 1.2 1.3 2.1 2.2 2.3 3.1 3.2 3.3 4.1 4.2 4.3
Read the full JRC use cases (pp. 38–41)

Self-check

A short, optional knowledge check — match real situations to the right GreenComp competence. No score, no pressure: it just helps the framework stick.

NA Aggregator

Drop participant CSVs to visualise Pre/Post-TCA impact across your group.

How NA-aggregation works

The toolkit has no central server (single-file by design). But you can still aggregate group data — here's the workflow:

1
Share the link/file with participants 2 weeks before TCA. They open the toolkit (no install, no login).
→
2
Participants self-rate, export CSV Save Pre-TCA snapshot, click Export CSV in header, mail to you.
→
3
After TCA: collect Post-CSVs Same workflow: rate again, export, mail to NA.
→
4
Drop them here, see impact Radar + per-area deltas + biggest movers. Export consolidated report.
🔒 GDPR-compliant No upload — all CSVs are read locally in your browser. Anonymise filenames before sharing reports externally.
📥 No backend needed The toolkit is a single HTML file — perfect for offline-first NAs. No server costs, no infrastructure.
📊 Use for EU strategic-priority reporting The Pre/Post-data documents green-skill development — supports alignment with the Erasmus+ Green Strategy 2024 and the Union of Skills priority (Commission, March 2025).
Try the aggregator with realistic sample data before your real TCA
Drop participant CSVs

Each participant returns their CSV (Assessment tab → Export) or a filled-in PDF form. Drop multiple files here to aggregate Pre + Post snapshots automatically. All processing stays in your browser — no upload.

Drop CSV or filled-PDF files here or click to browse
Multi-select · .csv or filled .pdf forms

GreenComp in EU Policy

Interactive timeline of policy anchors — click any milestone to see references, links, and how GreenComp fits.

EU Policy Timeline
Policy anchors · click to expand
EU 2025+ strategic priorities
🛠️ Union of Skills
Build workforce competences (incl. green, digital, social).
2.1 Systems Thinking 3.1 Futures Literacy 3.3 Individual Initiative
🛡️ Preparedness Union
Build societal resilience to crises (climate, security, health).
3.2 Adaptability 4.1 Political Agency 4.2 Collective Action
Take-away · The 12 competences map cleanly to BOTH pillars. Any TCA built on GreenComp can be reported under either.

How snapshots work

Workshop Agenda

Configure the three parameters, then generate. The tool picks methods that match your TCA Plan competences, sequences them along the energy curve, and fits everything to your duration and group size.

Custom workshop method

Add a workshop you've run before. It joins the library with your built-in methods, and the Workshop Agenda picks it up too. Fields marked * are required.

About this toolkit

AIBA GreenComp Toolkit v10.1
EU sustainability competence framework · for NAs and TCA participants
12
competences
17
workshop methods
15
priorities & TCA themes
10
TCA scenarios

What this toolkit does

It operationalises GreenComp — the EU sustainability competence framework — for the people who actually use it: National Agencies running TCAs, participants in those TCAs, and project beneficiaries writing applications. Everything happens in your browser. Nothing is uploaded. Nothing is tracked.

Key features

  • ✓ 12 competences — official JRC descriptions & KSA statements word-for-word (EN/DE), behavioural anchors per level, reflection questions, examples
  • ✓ 17 facilitator-ready workshop methods · multi-select · print-ready export
  • ✓ TCA Mapping with 4 official priorities + 11 TCA themes + 10 scenarios + Activity Designer + Coverage Score
  • ✓ Self-assessment (Pre/Post snapshots) · results PDF · fillable PDF form · free-text feedback · CSV + SHA-256 transparency pack
  • ✓ One-click send to NA — your results PDF attached via the share sheet (phones & modern browsers), or download + pre-addressed mail; CSV/.eml under Advanced
  • ✓ Share methods & reflection questions between NAs — small .json files, one-click import
  • ✓ NA Aggregator (reads CSV or filled PDF forms) · radar · table · biggest movers · per-area deltas · participant feedback
  • ✓ 2 personas (NA · Participant) with adaptive UI
  • ✓ Bilingual (DE/EN) · Dark/Light theme · Fully offline · GDPR-safe

Quick start by persona

The toolkit serves two roles. Each has a clear primary tab and a clear secondary one. Switch your role via the badge top-right.

NA (organiser) · Primary: Plan → TCA Mapping (scenarios, priorities, sectors → competences → workshop). Then Aggregator (drop participant CSVs → radar + table + movers + NA report). Transparency pack from header for self-verifiable data trail.
Participant (TCA attendee) · Primary: Assessment (rate 1–5, save Pre/After-TCA snapshots — the save bar repeats at the bottom). Save your results as PDF, download a fillable PDF form, leave feedback, then send your PDF to your NA in one click — on phones and modern browsers the share sheet attaches the PDF automatically. Secondary: Framework (read the 12 competences with KSA + behavioural anchors).

Hidden gems · features you might miss

Powerful features that are tucked away. Each is a click — explore them.

Gallery tab · 12 spotlight cards · Each competence shows the official JRC example plus one signature action you can do today. Click a gem to dive into the full competence detail.
Behavioural anchors per Level 1–5 · Click any competence card → 'What does each level look like?' shows concrete observable behaviours for every level. Click the number to set your rating directly.
Activity Designer (TCA tab) · Pick competences for your TCA Plan, then generate a full workshop sequence — matched methods, learning objectives, energy curve. Adjust duration + group size for instant re-plan.
Transparency Pack with SHA-256 · Header button next to Export CSV. Generates a single HTML with all your toolkit data + SHA-256 integrity hashes computed in-browser via the Web Crypto API. Anyone with the file can recompute the hashes to self-verify nothing changed. Useful for transparent NA reporting — not a substitute for formal third-party certification.
Aggregator 6-tab view · Drop participant CSVs (or click 'Sample CSV') and the Aggregator gives you Overview, Radar, By-area, Table, Biggest Movers and Export — each its own tab. Print or download an NA report.
Scenario library (TCA tab) · 10 ready-made TCA scenarios from real NAs (VET Vienna, Climate Justice Lisbon, Whole-School Helsinki…). One click loads all relevant competences into your TCA Plan.
Print-ready exports everywhere · Methods can be multi-selected and exported as a printable HTML. TCA Plans export with full competence descriptions. NA Reports export as styled documents — all open in a new tab and trigger the print dialog automatically.
Share methods & questions between NAs · Select methods and click 'Share methods (.json)' — or share your reflection questions from the Workshop Agenda. Drop the small file in Teams or a shared folder; colleagues import it with one click.

Keyboard + tips

Save the file first · Got the toolkit by e-mail? Save it to a fixed place (Desktop or Documents) and always open that copy — don't work in the e-mail preview. Your entries are stored by your browser on this device, tied to where the file lives.
ESC closes any modal or tour · In Tour: ← → to navigate. Any open dropdown (persona, app-switcher) closes with ESC.
Info icons everywhere (?) · Look for the small round question-mark next to section titles — click for a contextual explanation.
Bilingual + theme · EN/DE switch in header changes all UI text. Light/Dark theme toggle in the Suite-Bar (top header). Both persist across visits via localStorage.
Reset = clean slate · The Reset button (header, or About → Tech & Privacy) wipes all your data — ratings, snapshots, plan, reflections and saved sessions. Any custom language you added is kept. Ideal when handing the tool to a new user.

Got feedback?

The Send feedback button (footer) opens a pre-filled email — what works well, what doesn't, ideas. English is always fine. Every reply shapes the next version.

Michael Kolbe
Head of Adult Education & Corporate Communication · AIBA Liechtenstein
michael.kolbe@aiba.li LinkedIn

Add your own language

The toolkit ships in English and German. Any National Agency can add a third language — Spanish, Turkish, Polish, any language — without touching the code. Three steps:

1
Download the Excel/CSV template
A spreadsheet with every English label in column A. Opens directly in Excel, Numbers or Google Sheets.
2
Translate in column B
Type your translation next to each English phrase. Fill the language code (e.g. tr) and name (e.g. Türkçe) into the two marked rows at the top. Leave a cell empty to keep English.
3
Save as CSV and upload it back
The new language appears as a third button next to EN / DE. It is saved in your browser.
📊 Editing in Excel — keep it clean: Open the file, fill ONLY column B, then File → Save As and keep the CSV format. Umlauts (ä, ç, ñ) and dashes are handled automatically — don't change the encoding. If columns or characters ever look wrong, use Data → From Text/CSV and pick “Unicode (UTF-8)” + semicolon; or simply use Google Sheets / Numbers, which always read it correctly.

Single-file architecture

The entire toolkit is one HTML file (~1.9 MB). All code, content, translations, fonts, the PDF engine and visualisations are inlined. No build step, no server, no dependencies, no JavaScript frameworks.

100% offline, 100% private

Works fully offline — all code, fonts and content are embedded in this single HTML file. Nothing ever leaves your browser. Your ratings, snapshots, plan and persona live in localStorage on your device.

GDPR (DSGVO) compliance

  • ✓ No server, no upload, no tracking, no cookies, no analytics, no telemetry
  • ✓ All data stays on the user's device — localStorage only
  • ✓ Transparency Pack uses SHA-256 integrity hashes (computed in-browser via Web Crypto API) for self-verifiable data trails — not a substitute for formal audit certification
  • ✓ Reset wipes all your data on this device instantly

Removes all your ratings, snapshots, plan, reflections, feedback and saved sessions on this device. Any custom language or methods you added are kept. This cannot be undone.

Browser support

Modern Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari (last 2 years). Mobile-friendly. Print stylesheets included for paper output.

Framework source

GreenComp · The European sustainability competence framework

Bianchi, G., Pisiotis, U., Cabrera Giraldez, M. (2022). GreenComp · The European sustainability competence framework. Punie, Y. and Bacigalupo, M. editors. Publications Office of the European Union, Luxembourg. JRC128040.

→ JRC publication page

Tool licence

The AIBA GreenComp Toolkit is free to use, share and adapt for any non-commercial Erasmus+ / ESC purpose, with attribution to AIBA Liechtenstein. The GreenComp framework itself is © European Union 2022, used under Creative Commons.
